programme.title: ABBA, The Carpenters & Dolly Parton: The Unseen Tapes
programme.desc: ABBA, The Carpenters and Dolly Parton were three of the world's most successful musical acts who rose to prominence during the early 1970s. This special programme features rare performance footage of all three, previously unseen by British viewers. Featured in the programme are ABBA's hits Mamma Mia, Dancing Queen, SOS, Waterloo and Fernando. The Carpenters were America's biggest selling artists of the Seventies, and featured are performances of Close to You, Top of the World and Superstar. And Dolly Parton's rise to superstardom began when she broadened her fanbase beyond country music. Featured are performances of Jolene, I Will Always Love You and Here You Come Again. Featuring analysis from Tony Blackburn, Johnny Logan, Glen Matlock, Mike Batt, Paul Gambaccini, Joan Armatrading, Bob Harris and Ben Earle. Exec Prod: Mark Wells; Dir: Mark Wells; Double Yellow Entertainment

programme.title: Dave Allen: In His Own Words
programme.desc: Dave Allen was one of the most influential comedians to appear on British TV, a brilliant storyteller whose sharp intelligence and mischievous irreverence changed TV comedy. But his humour often came under fire from all directions from the tabloids to the Church. He was even banned outright for what called harmless, naughty little sketches. This celebration of the comic features contributions from long-time collaborators Jacquie Clarke and Sabina Franklyn, alongside Jimmy Tarbuck, John Lloyd, Paul Jackson and comedians inspired by Dave's trailblazing voice - such as Stewart Lee and Adil Ray. This is a warm, funny and revealing celebration of a comic who made generations laugh - and taught us to look at the world differently. Exec Prod: Ned Parker/Danny Tipping; Dir: Colin Burgess; Longwave Studios

Alexis Conran investigates some weight loss meal plans with the help of the air fryer.

programme.title: Tom Read Wilson's Magnificent Journey
programme.desc: This series reimagines the rite of passage once undertaken by 17th, 18th and 19th-century aristocrats on their Grand Tours of Europe. Craving a touch of cultural enlightenment, Tom Read Wilson sets out to follow in their footsteps. He begins in Paris, where he's introduced to the refined world of aristocratic etiquette under the guidance of a Parisian contessa, before visiting some of the city's most culturally significant sites including Notre-Dame and the Gardens of Versailles. He then continues his journey south, travelling by horse and carriage, and later by train, to arrive at Lake Annecy, where he checks into a lakeside hotel frequented by the Grand Tourists. Exec Prods: Ros Edwards/Ed Taylor; Dir: Alexis Girardet; Honey Bee Media Ltd (S1 Ep1/3)

programme.title: Jane McDonald: Pole to Pole
programme.desc: Jane McDonald's on a mission to step out of her comfort zone, pushing herself beyond her limits to travel to the ends of the earth in search of bucket-list adventures - crossing oceans, continents and time zones from Antarctica to the Arctic. Her journey takes in the Falkland Islands and South Georgia, Uruguay, Brazil, Greece and Italy before finally reaching Norway, Svalbard and the Arctic Circle, travelling by yacht, plane, helicopter, rib boat and even kayak in search of incredible experiences. In this episode - the Antarctic peninsula. It's as close to the South Pole as Jane can get and the beginning of her epic expedition. Jane takes a ride around Winter Island, getting up close with icebergs and the native wildlife; braves the dangerous Antarctic waters in a two person kayak; Jane explores the mountainous glacial terrain of Livingstone Island on foot, coming face to face with elephant seals; and tries aerial yoga. Exec Prods: Kieron Collins/Mark Powell; Dir: Jenny MacLeod; JetSetTV Ltd (Ep1/6)

programme.title: Kinky Boots
programme.desc: British comedy drama. A drag queen comes to the rescue of a man who, after inheriting his father's shoe factory, needs to diversify his product if he wants to keep the business afloat.
